goal: Add input validation to the /read endpoint in the Go fileserver (main.go) before the filesystem call, without changing the existing query-parameter API contract.

tasks.name: Add validation to /read handler
tasks.action: In the /read HTTP handler, before the os.OpenRoot / file open call, add the following checks and return an appropriate HTTP error code for each failure: (1) reject a missing or empty `file_path` query parameter with 400 Bad Request; (2) reject any path that contains a null byte (`\x00`) with 400 Bad Request; (3) reject any path whose byte length exceeds 4096 characters with 400 Bad Request. Do NOT add any path-traversal string filtering — that is handled structurally by os.OpenRoot. Do NOT change the query-parameter name, the success response shape, or any other endpoint.
tasks.verify: Run `go build ./...` inside fileserver/ — it must compile with no errors.
tasks.done: main.go compiles cleanly and the /read handler contains all three guard clauses before the filesystem open call.

tasks.name: Add unit tests for the new validation
tasks.action: In mcp_test.go, add test cases that exercise the three new validation paths for the /read endpoint: (1) a request with no `file_path` parameter returns 400; (2) a request where `file_path` contains a null byte returns 400; (3) a request where `file_path` is a 4097-byte string returns 400. Keep all existing tests passing. Follow the naming and structure conventions already used in the file.
tasks.verify: Run `go test ./...` inside fileserver/ — all tests (existing + new) must pass with exit code 0.
tasks.done: go test reports PASS and the three new test cases are present in mcp_test.go.
